entity: Remote acquisition and purchase of cell phone location information
description: Using cell site simulators and commercially purchased cell phone location data—such as the data that powers geographically targeted advertisements —DHS can determine all the devices that were in a certain location at a given time (e.g. at a protest) and track specific devices over time, revealing frequented addresses, associations, and other intimate life details.

entity: License plate readers (LPR) and LPR data
description: Mounted at fixed location or installed on vehicles, LPRs record the license plate and location of vehicles that pass by, enabling DHS to determine the precise location of vehicles over time. DHS both operates its own LPRs and obtains data from commercial or other government-operated LPR systems. Agents can use this to track criminal suspects, immigrants, or protestors.

sourceLinks.label: AP: Border Patrol Is Monitoring U.S. Drivers and Detaining Those with ‘Suspicious’ Travel Patterns (Nov. 2025)

description: By attaching GPS monitors or using digital forensics to extract information from on-board computers, DHS can access detailed information about where cars have been.
